idea-plugin.name: CIMcheck
idea-plugin.description:
Real-time SPARQL and SHACL validation against CIM/CGMES schema profiles, powered by the CIMcheck language server. Write a SPARQL query or a SHACL shape and get immediate feedback as you type: unknown classes and properties are underlined, syntax errors are highlighted, and semantic issues such as domain/range mismatches are flagged — all resolved against your actual RDFS profile files.

Features

  • Syntax highlighting for SPARQL (.rq, .sparql) and SHACL / Turtle (.ttl, .shacl).
  • Real-time diagnostics — unknown terms, datatype mismatches, properties not allowed for a class, cardinality errors and more.
  • Hover documentation — the full IRI and source profile for any CIM term (e.g. cim:ACLineSegment).
  • Auto-completion — type : after a prefix such as cim: for class and property suggestions.
  • Go to definition — Ctrl+Click / Cmd+Click a CIM term to jump to its declaration in the profile file.
  • Workspace symbol search — press Ctrl+Alt+Shift+N (Go to Symbol) and type a term name to find any schema term.

Requirements

  • IntelliJ 2024.2 or later. The plugin runs the language server on the IDE's bundled Java runtime, which is Java 21+ from 2024.2 onward.
  • The LSP4IJ plugin. When you install CIMcheck from the Marketplace it is pulled in automatically; if you install CIMcheck from a downloaded .zip, install LSP4IJ manually first (Settings → Plugins → Marketplace → search "LSP4IJ").
  • A Java 21+ runtime. By default the plugin uses the IDE's own runtime; you can point it at a different one in settings.

Getting started

  1. Install LSP4IJ if prompted — it is a required dependency and provides the LSP client.
  2. Create a configuration file at .cgmes/validation.json in your project root. This tells CIMcheck where your RDFS profile files are: 
    {
  "schemasDirectory": "schemas/cgmes-3.0"
}
    or list individual files: 
    {
  "schemas": ["schemas/EquipmentCore.rdf", "schemas/Topology.rdf"]
}
    The file is watched and the schema reloads automatically whenever it changes.
  3. Open a .rq, .sparql, .ttl or .shacl file. The server starts, loads the schema in the background, and begins validating.

Settings

Under Settings / Preferences → Tools → CIMcheck:

  • Server JAR — leave empty to use the JAR bundled with the plugin, or point to your own cimcheck-lsp.jar.
  • Java executable — leave empty to use the IDE's runtime, or set a path to a Java 21+ executable.
  • JVM arguments — extra arguments passed before -jar, e.g. -Xmx512m.

Validation configuration

.cgmes/validation.json also supports strictness (permissive / default / strict / pedantic), namedGraphs (map graph IRIs to profile versions) and prefixes (default PREFIX declarations injected into queries). See the project README for the full reference.

Troubleshooting

  • No syntax highlighting / file not recognised — confirm the file extension is one of .rq, .sparql, .ttl, .shacl. If a previously installed plugin already claimed .ttl, add the association under Settings → Editor → File Types → SHACL.
  • No diagnostics appearing — make sure .cgmes/validation.json exists in the project root and that LSP4IJ is enabled. Open the Language Servers tool window (from LSP4IJ) to see CIMcheck's status and logs.
  • Server fails to start — usually a Java version problem. Set Settings → Tools → CIMcheck → Java executable to a Java 21+ path.
